sourceMovie.title: Detachment
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Detachment for the same reason it broke through in 2011: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Tony Kaye keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Dead Poets Society
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detachment clicked for you, Dead Poets Society (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Holdovers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detachment clicked for you, The Holdovers (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Detachment?
faq.answer: Try Dead Poets Society, The Holdovers, Aftersun—each shares DNA with Detachment while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detachment?
faq.answer: Detachment (2011) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detachment worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
